item.title: WTO chief: world order has irrevocably changed
item.description: The head of the World вҖӢTrade Organization said on Thursday the multilateral system вҖҢhas fundamentally changed and that countries must look to the future to consider how to reform the global trade system.

item.title: Pornhub, Stripchat, XNXX, XVideos charged with breaching EU tech rules, risk fines
item.description: Adult content platforms Pornhub, Stripchat, XNXX and XVideos have been charged with breaching EU rules вҖӢby letting children access pornographic content on their sites, EU вҖҢregulators said on Thursday, which could lead to hefty fines.

item.title: Snapchat hit with EU probe into alleged failure to prevent child grooming, illegal goods sales
item.description: Snap-owned Snapchat was hit with an EU investigation on Thursday as regulators warned that вҖӢthe social platform appears not to be doing вҖҢenough to prevent child grooming and the sale of illegal goods.

item.title: Wall Street bets US corporate earnings will withstand surging oil prices
item.description: Wall Street is confident that strong corporate earnings will prop up stock prices that have slumped since the Iran war began and set oil prices surging, rekindling worries about inflation.

item.title: Morning Bid: Reality check
item.description: What matters in U.S. and global markets today

item.title: OpenAI indefinitely pauses plans to release erotic chatbot, FT says
item.description: OpenAI вҖӢhas shelved plans to вҖҢrelease an erotic chatbot indefinitely as it looks to вҖӢfocus on its core вҖӢproducts, according to a report вҒ by the Financial Times вҖӢon Thursday, citing people вҖӢfamiliar with the matter.

item.title: OECD: Iran war erases global growth upgrade, fans inflation
item.description: The escalating conflict in the Middle East has knocked the global economy off a вҖҢstronger growth path, the OECD warned on Thursday, as a near-halt in energy shipments through the Strait of Hormuz threatens to push inflation sharply higher.

item.title: China's Pony.ai to more than double robotaxi fleet, debuts in Zagreb
item.description: Pony.ai expects to more than double the fleet of robotaxis powered by its technology to over 3,000 units вҖӢacross more than 20 cities globally this year, the вҖҢGuangzhou-based firm said on Thursday as it announced its first-ever quarterly profit.

item.title: UK sanctions Cambodia-based scam centre and crypto platform
item.description: Britain on Thursday sanctioned the operators of what it described as the largest fraud compound in Cambodia and вҖӢan online crypto marketplace used to trade stolen personal вҖҢdata, in a bid to protect people in the UK from organised online scams.

item.title: An Indiana town shows two sides of Trump's factory "boom"
item.description: In industrial communities like South Bend, government policies are lifting some sectors while clouding the outlook for others, leaving many manufacturers navigating a patchwork of incentives, tariffs and shifting signals from Washington.

item.title: Insight: How the Trump administration is testing Fed independence on bank rules
item.description: While U.S. President Donald Trump has been brazen in his demands for the historically independent Federal Reserve to lower interest rates, his administration is also trying to quietly steer the central bank's policy in another critical area: oversight of Wall Street banks.

item.title: US jury verdicts against Meta, Google tee up fight over tech liability shield
item.description: Jurors in the first two trials in the U.S. from a growing wave of lawsuits targeting social media firms over harm to children вҖӢhave found Meta and Alphabet's Google liable, potentially teeing up an appeals fight that could reshape how U.S. law shields tech companies from lawsuits.

item.title: US stock futures slip as Middle East war de-escalation remains uncertain
item.description: U.S. stock index вҖӢfutures were down on Thursday, following gains in the previous session, as investors вҖҢremained cautious over developments in the Middle East while weighing the prospects of a de-escalation.

item.title: From beer to cosmetics, Asia feels full force of war-fuelled energy crisis
item.description: From beer and crisps to noodles, toys and cosmetics, companies - and consumers - across Asia are bracing for a crisis as the Iran war wreaks havoc on supply chains, plastics and oil supplies, upending everyday life and sending prices soaring.

item.title: Korean Air plans 103 Boeing plane purchases valued at $36.2 bln until 2039
item.description: South Korean вҖӢflag carrier Korean вҖҢAir said on Thursday вҖӢit plans вҖӢto buy 103 вҒ Boeing planes вҖӢbetween 2026 вҖӢand 2039.

item.title: US lawmakers to introduce bill to ban government use of Chinese robots
item.description: Two U.S. senators on Thursday planned to introduce a bill that would ban the government from buying or operating humanoid robots made вҖӢby Chinese firms.

item.title: Wall Street bonuses surge 9% to record $49.2 billion in 2025, NY comptroller says
item.description: Bonuses for Wall Street executives jumped 9% to a record $49.2 billion вҖӢin 2025, according to an estimate from New вҖҢYork State Comptroller Tom DiNapoli published on Thursday.

item.title: South Korea to invest $166 million in AI chip startup Rebellions
item.description: South Korea's industry ministry on Tuesday said the Financial Services Commission's advisory board вҖӢapproved a 250 billion won ($166 million) investment вҖҢin a local artificial intelligence chip startup called Rebellions, part of a government-backed push to nurture a homegrown advanced semiconductor вҖӢfirm.

item.title: Rohm, Toshiba, Mitsubishi Elec to begin power chip integration talks, Nikkei says
item.description: Japan's Rohm , Toshiba вҖӢand Mitsubishi Electric вҖҢwill start talks to integrate вҖӢtheir power вҖӢsemiconductor businesses that can вҒ form the вҖӢworld's second-biggest power вҖӢchip group after Infineon , the Nikkei вҖӢnewspaper said вҖӢon Thursday.

item.title: Google top India counsel quits in latest departure amid regulatory hurdles, sources say
item.description: Google's top India counsel, Bijoya Roy, has resigned after 16 months вҖӢin the role, two sources said, a вҖҢhigh-profile exit in a key market where the U.S. tech giant is facing regulatory hurdles and also lacks a вҖӢgovernment relations head.

item.title: Uber, Pony.ai and Verne team up to launchВ Europe's first robotaxi service in Croatia
item.description: Uber Technologies has partnered with Pony.ai and autonomous vehicle startup Verne to roll out the first commercial вҖӢrobotaxi service in Europe, with operations set to вҖҢstart in the Croatian capital Zagreb.

item.title: Exclusive: China considers easing bank shareholding limits to boost capital
item.description: China is considering easing shareholding restrictions for some major investors, people with knowledge of the matter said, in a move aimed at broadening capital-raising options for commercial banks reeling вҖӢfrom an economic slowdown.

item.title: Hair care brand Olaplex to be acquired by Germany's Henkel for $1.4 billion
item.description: Hair вҖӢcare brand вҖҢOlaplex Holdings вҖӢon вҖӢThursday said вҒ it вҖӢentered into вҖӢa definitive agreement вҖӢto вҖӢbe acquired вҖҢby вҒ Germany consumer goods вҖӢmaker вҖӢHenkel вҒ for $1.4 billion.

item.title: Australia's Qantas boosts Europe flight as demand climbs due to Mideast war
item.description: Australia's flag carrier Qantas Airways QAN.AX said on Thursday it is adding flights to Rome and Paris to meet an вҖӢupswing in demand for European routes, while global air вҖҢtravel remains severely disrupted after the Middle East war forced the closure of major hubs, including Dubai and Doha.

item.title: Volkswagen owner Porsche SE reports blow to 2025 earnings
item.description: Porsche SE , which вҖӢcontrols German вҖҢauto group Volkswagen , suffered a вҖӢ9% вҖӢblow to its after-tax вҒ profit in вҖӢ2025, the вҖӢcompany said on Wednesday.

item.title: H&M's Q1 profit grows more than expected, sees March sales up 1%
item.description: Swedish fashion retailer H&M reported вҖӢon Thursday a slightly bigger rise than expected in вҖҢDecember-February operating profit, and predicted March sales would be up 1% in local currencies.

item.title: National Australia Bank to cut 170 jobs amid offshore expansion
item.description: Australia's Finance Sector Union said on Thursday that National Australia Bank is proposing cutting about 170 jobs across the country вҖӢas part of a restructuring of its business division, which вҖҢwill also create new roles offshore.

item.title: Morning Bid: Hope and Hormuz
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Ankur Banerjee

item.title: Japan shifts focus to oil in unorthodox scramble to talk up yen
item.description: Japan is weighing a controversial plan to arrest the yen's slide: stepping into oil futures markets, sources say, as long-standing policy tools lose traction вҖӢagainst stubborn inflation pressures.

item.title: Revolut to base 40% of its global workforce in India by 2026
item.description: European fintech firm Revolut plans to have around 40% of its global workforce based in India by the end of 2026 as вҖӢit expands its India global capability centre.
